messages.content:
Thread Title: CW4Kids Lineup Changing Back to Early 2007 Kids WB?! Anyone Else Seeing This?

AnimeFan93:

Hey everyone,

So, this Saturday morning, I turned on CW4Kids expecting the usual lineup, but to my surprise, the schedule was straight out of early 2007 Kids WB! I’m not sure if it’s some kind of glitch, a special event, or a permanent change, but it was a total blast from the past.

Here’s what the lineup looked like before, from 7am to 12pm:

7:00 AM - Yu-Gi-Oh! 5Ds
7:30 AM - Viva Pinata 
8:00 AM - GoGoRiki
8:30 AM - The Spectacular Spider Man
9:00 AM - Chaotic 
9:30 AM - Huntik: Secrets and Seekers
10:00 AM - Sonic X
10:30 AM - Kirby: Right Back At Ya
11:00 AM - Dinosaur King
11:30 AM - TMNT: Fast Forward

And here’s what aired today:

7:00 AM - Loonatics Unleashed
7:30 AM - Xiaolin Showdown
8:00 AM - Johnny Test
8:30 AM - Legion of Super Heroes
9:00 AM - The Batman
9:30 AM - Shaggy & Scooby-Doo Get a Clue!
10:00 AM - Tom and Jerry Tales
10:30 AM - Monster Allergy
11:00 AM - Spider Riders
11:30 AM - Magi-Nation

Has anyone else experienced this? Is it happening nationwide, or just on my local station? I checked the official CW4Kids website and their social media, but there’s no mention of reverting to the old Kids WB lineup. Wondering if it’s a technical glitch or maybe a surprise nostalgia event.

Would love to hear if this is happening elsewhere!

ToonLover105:

Wow, that’s crazy, AnimeFan93! I can’t believe it. Haven’t seen those shows on TV in years. I’m elsewhere in the country, but all we got was the regular CW4Kids schedule this morning. Sounds like a fun glitch or a really cool event. I hope it’s something they do more frequently if it’s intentional!

RetroFan88:

Nothing like that in my area, but man, what I wouldn’t give to see Legion of Super Heroes and The Batman air again. It sounds like you hit a jackpot, AnimeFan93! Maybe it’s a test run for a nostalgia block, or perhaps a one-off thing? Either way, super jealous here.

DigitalWatcher101:

I’m a bit of a skeptic here - could it be that your TV guide was just not updated or glitched out? Sometimes, my TV does weird things, and I swear I’m seeing content that isn’t supposed to be there. Although, the lineup you described does sound like an awesome Saturday morning.

NostalgiaNut45:

No way! They brought back Monster Allergy?! That’s one of those underrated shows I never thought I’d see on air again. I need to check my local listings and hope for a miracle here too. AnimeFan93, keep us posted if it happens again next weekend!

AnimeFan93:

Thanks for the responses, everyone! I thought I was losing it for a moment there. I’ll be sure to check again next Saturday and let you all know if the classic lineup airs once more or if it was just a one-time miracle. Fingers crossed it happens again – wouldn’t mind reliving those childhood Saturday mornings a bit longer!
Write a follow up update the next week

Thread Update: Did the CW4Kids Classic Lineup Air Again? Here's What Happened!

AnimeFan93:

Hey, everyone!

As promised, I’m back with an update about the CW4Kids/early 2007 Kids WB lineup situation. So, I got up bright and early again this past Saturday, eagerly awaiting to see if last week's flashback was a one-off event or something more. Sadly, it looks like it was just a temporary glitch in the matrix—this week reverted back to the usual CW4Kids schedule:

7:00 AM - Yu-Gi-Oh! 5Ds
7:30 AM - Viva Pinata
8:00 AM - GoGoRiki
8:30 AM - The Spectacular Spider-Man
9:00 AM - Chaotic
9:30 AM - Huntik: Secrets and Seekers
10:00 AM - Sonic X
10:30 AM - Kirby: Right Back At Ya
11:00 AM - Dinosaur King
11:30 AM - TMNT: Fast Forward

I was really hoping that last week's lineup was a deliberate nostalgic event or perhaps a teaser for something bigger, but no such luck. This week, everything was back to normal with no sign of the classic Kids WB lineup. I did reach out to my local CW station and they mentioned a "technical scheduling error" last week, which led to the unexpected lineup. So, unfortunately, it doesn't look like a nostalgia block is in the works—at least not officially.

ToonLover105:

That’s a bummer, AnimeFan93! But hey, at least you got to experience that blast from the past, even if it was just for one week. Maybe the positive reaction to the glitch (at least from us nostalgic fans) might encourage them to consider a real throwback event in the future.

RetroFan88:

Total letdown, but thanks for keeping us in the loop, AnimeFan93. I guess it’s back to streaming services and DVDs to catch our old favorites. Still, fingers crossed that the powers-that-be see there’s interest and give us a dedicated nostalgia block one of these days.

DigitalWatcher101:

Mystery solved, then. Gotta love when technology throws us a curveball like that. At least it was a fun, albeit accidental, trip down memory lane for those who got to witness it!

NostalgiaNut45:

Thanks for updating us, AnimeFan93. A bit sad it was just a technical error, but what a cool error to have, right? Maybe we should start a petition or something for a classic Saturday morning block – I bet there’s more of us out there who’d love this to become a reality.

AnimeFan93:

Appreciate all the responses and empathy, folks! I'm glad I wasn't alone in feeling the excitement. Maybe if there's enough buzz, something good will come out of it. And NostalgiaNut45, I like the way you think—a petition could be interesting. Let's keep the hope alive and our fingers crossed. For now, though, it’s back to finding our beloved classics through other means. Catch you all around, and here’s to more pleasant surprises in the future!
Write a follow up where they'd swap the current lineup for the KWB one, but they'd miss Smeshariki/GoGoRiki and Sonic X,

Thread Update: Surprising Turn of Events - Classic KWB Lineup Returns, But Not Without Losses

AnimeFan93:

Hey, everyone!

Remember the unexpected return of the classic Kids WB lineup on CW4Kids a couple of weekends ago, followed by the disappointing revelation that it was just a "technical scheduling error"? Well, there's been a surprising turn of events that I think many of you will find interesting.

This past Saturday, I geared up for my usual weekend cartoon binge, half expecting the regular CW4Kids schedule after the previous letdown. To my total astonishment, the classic Kids WB lineup was BACK! Only this time, it appears intentional, as the local station ran teasers hyping the return of our beloved shows. Here's the kicker, though: amidst the excitement of seeing shows like "Legion of Super Heroes," "The Batman," and "Xiaolin Showdown" back on air, I realized that "Smeshariki/GoGoRiki" and "Sonic X" were notably missing from the lineup.

Instead, the lineup from 7am to 12pm was as follows:

7:00 AM - Loonatics Unleashed
7:30 AM - Xiaolin Showdown
8:00 AM - Johnny Test
8:30 AM - Legion of Super Heroes
9:00 AM - The Batman
9:30 AM - Shaggy & Scooby-Doo Get a Clue!
10:00 AM - Tom and Jerry Tales
10:30 AM - Monster Allergy
11:00 AM - Spider Riders
11:30 AM - Magi-Nation

While it's thrilling to have the classic KWB shows back, the absence of "Smeshariki/GoGoRiki" and "Sonic X" did not go unnoticed. It's a bittersweet change; while we celebrate the return of many childhood favorites, fans of "Sonic X" and the quirky world of "Smeshariki" are left wanting.

ToonLover105:

Wow, I never thought they'd actually bring the KWB lineup back for real! It’s amazing news, but missing "Sonic X" is a tough pill to swallow. That show was a huge part of my Saturday mornings. Maybe they’re planning to rotate some shows in and out?

RetroFan88:

Incredible! This has got to be one of the best surprises this year. Crossing my fingers they'll find a way to bring "Sonic X" back, though. It deserves a spot in that nostalgic lineup too.

DigitalWatcher101:

Guess the positive feedback they received (and maybe our confusion over the "technical glitch"?) prompted them to make the switch. It's awesome, but I have to agree about missing "GoGoRiki" and "Sonic X." Hopefully, those shows will pop up on the schedule at some point or maybe even on a streaming service.

NostalgiaNut45:

Honestly, I celebrate the return of the classic lineup, but "Sonic X" not being there is more disappointing than I thought it would be. Still, let's enjoy what we have and keep voicing our support for those missing titles!

AnimeFan93:

Absolutely, everyone. It's a mix of emotions, but overall, this return feels like a victory for nostalgia fans. Let's hope that with enough interest and support, we might see a more inclusive rotation that brings back "Sonic X" and "GoGoRiki." For now, Saturdays are once again a portal to childhood for many of us. Let's keep the conversation going and maybe, just maybe, our combined voices will bring about even more positive changes!
Write a follow up where they check the schedule for the next few weeks on their station, and the week later it appears permanent, (rip Pogoriki and Chikoriki)

Thread Update: Classic KWB Lineup Seems Permanent, But at a Cost

AnimeFan93:

Hello again, nostalgic warriors!

The past couple of weeks have been quite the rollercoaster for us fans of the classic Kids WB lineup on CW4Kids. Since my last update where I shared the exciting yet bittersweet return of our beloved KWB shows—minus “Smeshariki/GoGoRiki” and “Sonic X”—I’ve been closely monitoring the station's schedule, hoping for signs of what’s to come.

Well, it appears I have news: the classic KWB lineup seems to be cemented as the new (or should I say, returned) normal for the foreseeable future. I've checked the schedule for the next few weeks, and it consistently features the same set of shows that replaced the more recent CW4Kids programming. Sadly, there's no indication that “Smeshariki/GoGoRiki” or “Sonic X” will be making a comeback, marking what seems to be a permanent removal (RIP Pogoriki and Chikoriki).

Here’s the confirmed lineup for the next several weeks:

7:00 AM - Loonatics Unleashed
7:30 AM - Xiaolin Showdown
8:00 AM - Johnny Test
8:30 AM - Legion of Super Heroes
9:00 AM - The Batman
9:30 AM - Shaggy & Scooby-Doo Get a Clue!
10:00 AM - Tom and Jerry Tales
10:30 AM - Monster Allergy
11:00 AM - Spider Riders
11:30 AM - Magi-Nation

It’s a mixed bag of emotions. On one hand, the commitment to bringing back these classics is something many of us dreamed about but never truly believed would happen. On the other hand, the exclusion of “Sonic X” and “Smeshariki/GoGoRiki” feels like a loss for fans who cherished those series as part of their childhood repertoire.

ToonLover105:

It’s official then? That’s incredible news for classic KWB fans! It does sting not seeing "Sonic X" in the lineup, though. Here’s to hoping they’ll still find a way to honor all parts of their legacy, including the shows we’re missing now.

RetroFan88:

Permanent, huh? I guess it’s time to celebrate the wins and mourn the losses. “Sonic X” and “Smeshariki/GoGoRiki” will be missed, but seeing the rest of the KWB classics holding strong gives me hope. Maybe fan petitions or letters could sway them in the future?

DigitalWatcher101:

Permanent changes are always a bit of a double-edged sword, aren't they? Thrilled for the return of so many classics, but it’s a shame about the ones left behind. Does anyone know if there’s another place to catch “Sonic X” or “Smeshariki/GoGoRiki” episodes?

NostalgiaNut45:

I’m over the moon that the KWB lineup is back for good, but definitely feeling a pinch for “Sonic X” and “Smeshariki.” They were such a big part of what made Saturday mornings special. Let’s not lose hope, though. Channels change, but fan demand can bring shows back from the vault!

AnimeFan93:

Your optimism and passion are what make this community amazing, everyone. While we enjoy the return of many KWB classics, let’s also keep the memory and love for “Sonic X” and “Smeshariki/GoGoRiki” alive in our discussions and fan efforts. Who knows what the future may bring if we continue to speak up about our favorites? Keep watching, keep hoping, and most importantly, keep sharing your thoughts and memories. Here’s to more nostalgic victories and possible comebacks!
Write a tweet where that affiliates website now has a KWB page with the show times, with a "Looking for CW4Kids FAQ"

🎉 Exciting news for all nostalgia lovers and classic cartoon fans! 🚀 Our affiliate's website now proudly hosts a dedicated Kids WB (KWB) page featuring the revamped lineup and showtimes. 📺✨ Dive back into your childhood favorites and relive the magic! 🌟

🔍 Also, if you're seeking info on the recent CW4Kids changes, don't miss our "Looking for CW4Kids FAQ" section. Get all your questions answered there! 🤔💡

Check it out now ➡️ [link to website]

#KidsWB #ClassicCartoons #Nostalgia #SaturdayMornings #FAQ
Write a tweet where a kid really misses GoGoRiki, Chaotic and Spider-Man, but here's their new favourite

Just tuned into the new #KidsWB lineup and oh man, do I miss #GoGoRiki, #Chaotic, and #SpiderMan! 😢 But, hey, discovering #MonsterAllergy has been a wild ride! 🌟 It's quickly becoming my new favorite. 📚✨ Adventure, mystery, and some epic monsters? Count me in! 🐉 #NewFaves #CartoonLove
Write a tweet where the parent says "Zick numbed my son's pain of losing Pogoriki, Tom Majors and SpiderMan"

Watching my son wave goodbye to #Pogoriki, Tom Majors, and #SpiderMan was tough 😔, but discovering Zick in #MonsterAllergy has truly numbed the pain! 🌈 He's found a new hero in Zick, whose adventures bring just as much joy and excitement. 🎉 #SilverLining #NewBeginnings #HeroicTales

Write an ad saying "Looking for Chaotic?, well it's no longer on this affiliate, To continue watching please look online to find an alternative CW affiliate that still carries the CW4Kids, or just watch it online on 4kids.tv, Or you could stay here and watch"

🌟 Miss the Thrills of Chaotic? 🌟

We understand that many of you have been dedicated fans of Chaotic, tuning in faithfully to catch the latest adventures. While Chaotic is no longer airing on our affiliate, your journey doesn't have to end here!

🔍 To keep up with the action and never miss an episode:
1. Check online for an alternative CW affiliate that continues to carry the CW4Kids lineup.
2. Dive into the digital world by visiting 4kids.tv, where the battles rage on and adventures await, easily accessible anytime, anywhere.

📺 Or, why not discover something new right here with us? Our lineup is full of exciting shows that are sure to captivate, entertain, and maybe even become your next favorite!

Stay with us for more adventures, laughter, and epic tales. Because the story never really ends; it just finds new beginnings. 🌈✨

#Chaotic #NewAdventures #4kidsTV #StayTuned
Write a forum convo where the new Kids WB has those ads from the affiliate, the Chaotic ad advises viewers to go online, but suggests staying for a different show

User1: Hey everyone, did anyone else see that ad from the affiliate about Chaotic no longer being aired? They're actually suggesting we go online to keep watching it but also hinting we should stick around for something else. Kind of a mixed message, don't you think?

User2: Yeah, I caught that too. It’s a bummer about Chaotic, but I get it. The landscape of TV is changing. At least they're providing alternatives. Curious about what other show they're pushing as a replacement though. Anyone has insights?

User3: I believe they're trying to funnel us towards Monster Allergy, based on the schedule changes and promotional content recently. It's all over their social media too. Haven't watched it yet; can anyone vouch for it?

User4: Monster Allergy, huh? I’ve seen a few episodes. It's quite engaging if you're into the whole monsters and mysteries vibe. Not exactly a direct substitute for Chaotic’s strategy and action, but it has its charm.

User5: Noticed the ad too. Definitely gave off a "sorry, but not sorry" vibe with the whole go online but stay here pitch. Gave Monster Allergy a chance since they seem to be pushing it hard. It’s actually not bad! Still miss Chaotic though.

User6: What gets me is the push towards online viewing. Seems like they're slowly trying to transition viewers to digital platforms. Smart, but also kind of sad to see traditional TV change this way.

User7: True, @User6. It's inevitable though. I’m just glad they offered an alternative with 4kids.tv. Still, the recommendation to stay and watch another show feels like they're trying to cushion the blow. I'll give Monster Allergy a shot. Who knows? It might just fill the Chaotic void a little.

User8: Anyone else think this might just open doors for more online-exclusive content? With Chaotic moving to digital, it could lead to other shows following suit. Might be an interesting shift for Kids WB content and how we engage with our favorite shows.

Admin: Great conversation everyone! Just a reminder to keep things respectful and open-minded. Changes like these can be hard, but they also bring new opportunities for discovering great content. If anyone decides to check out Monster Allergy, feel free to share your thoughts and reviews here. Let’s keep the community informed and engaged!

Write a script from the ad, with the affiliate being WADE (fake affiliate) 7 Las Vegas, suggesting the alternative methods to keep watching Chaotic, but at the end it morphs into an ad for another show, saying "If you're lucky, it might become your new fav, here on the revived Kids WB"

[Open with the WADE 7 Las Vegas logo. Upbeat music plays in the background. The scene transitions to vibrant clips of "Chaotic" showcasing intense battles and key characters.]

Narrator: [Enthusiastically] Hey, Chaotic fans! We know how much you love diving into the thrilling world of Perim, following the adventures of Tom and his friends!

[The music lowers slightly, and the screen transitions to a more somber tone with the WADE 7 logo appearing again.]

Narrator: [Gently] But as all good tales evolve, so does our lineup here at WADE 7 Las Vegas. While Chaotic won't be continuing with us...

[A brief pause occurs as the scene switches to a visually appealing graphic saying, “Don't Worry!” with web browser and mobile device animations showing the 4kids.tv website.]

Narrator: [Optimistically] You can still chase the action and keep your journey alive! Just head online to 4kids.tv for all things Chaotic. Immerse yourself in episodes, games, and more, whenever you want, wherever you are!

[The music picks up pace again, transitioning to a glimpse of another show, "Monster Allergy." Clips highlight exciting moments, mysterious creatures, and the protagonists Zick and Elena.]

Narrator: [Excitedly] And while we're on the topic of adventures, why not discover a new realm right here on WADE 7? Meet Zick, a young boy with extraordinary powers, and his fearless friend Elena in...

[A vibrant title card for "Monster Allergy" splashes across the screen, followed by more engaging clips from the show.]

Narrator: [With enthusiasm] "Monster Allergy!" Dive into a world where monsters are real, friendships are powerful, and adventures are endless. Who knows? It might just become your new favorite here on the revived Kids WB!

[The music swells as the scene transitions back to the WADE 7 Las Vegas logo against a backdrop of clips from the "Monster Allergy" series, creating an inviting atmosphere.]

Narrator: [Conclusively] So, whether you're exploring Perim online or embarking on new adventures with Zick and Elena, your next favorite story is waiting for you...

[The screen fades to black with the text “Where stories live forever. WADE 7 Las Vegas.”]

Narrator: [Warmly] Here, on the revived Kids WB, only on WADE 7 Las Vegas.

[End of Ad]
Write a tweet with the 4 shows getting ads, GoGoRiki viewers were funneled to Tom and Jerry, Yu-Gi-Oh 5Ds fans were funneled to Xiaolin Showdown

🌟 Big changes happening at #KidsWB! 📺 While we bid a fond farewell to #GoGoRiki & #YuGiOh5Ds, there's a world of adventure still awaiting! 🚀 GoGoRiki fans, get ready for the timeless antics in Tom and Jerry 🐱🐭, while Yu-Gi-Oh enthusiasts - it's time to unlock the mystical world of #XiaolinShowdown! 🐉✨ Let the new journeys begin! #TomAndJerry #AnimationAdventure
Write a follow up tweet targeted at Chaotic and Viva Pinata fans

🌀 Saying goodbye to #Chaotic & #VivaPinata is bittersweet, but the adventures don't stop here! 🌈 Chaotic fans, dive into the action-packed universe of #LegionOfSuperHeroes 🦸‍♂️🦸‍♀️, and for those who loved the vibrant world of Viva Pinata, get ready for the magical enchantment of #MagiNation! 🌟 Let's embrace these new worlds together! 💫 #NewAdventuresAwait
Write a tweet noting the 2 shows being pushed at ex Chaotic fans

🌌 Attention all #Chaotic fans - the adventure evolves! 🌀 While we may miss the realms of Perim, the journey continues with not one, but TWO epic shows being highlighted just for you. Dive into the superhero universe with #LegionOfSuperHeroes 🦸 and explore mystical realms in #MagiNation 🧙‍♂️✨. Get ready for double the action, double the magic, and endless new adventures! 🚀 #NewBeginnings #AdventureAwaits
Write an ad for fans of Huntik, saying Here's another, different show, but by the same people

🔍 Calling All #Huntik Fans! 🔍

While we cherish the thrilling quests and battles of #Huntik, we're excited to introduce you to a new horizon of adventure crafted by the same brilliant minds. 🌟

🐉 Dive into the world of #DragonQuest! 🛡️ A realm where courage meets destiny, and heroes are born from the legends whispered by the wind. Just like Huntik captured your imagination with its gripping tales, Dragon Quest promises an unforgettable journey of magic, mystery, and valor.

🎬 From the creators who brought legends to life, comes a story where every episode is a piece of a puzzle waiting to be solved, every character is a friend waiting to be met, and every moment is an adventure waiting to unfold.

🌈 Here's to discovering new worlds, embracing new dreams, and igniting new passions. Because the magic doesn't end; it transforms.

👀 Don't miss out! Turn the page to your next great adventure with #DragonQuest, only on our network. Where stories live forever, and every watcher is a seeker of wonders.

🌟 Join us. Let's embark on this new journey together. Here’s another different show, but by the same people who know how to tug at your heartstrings and set your spirit on an adventure!

#NewAdventures #SameCreators #MagicAwaits
